title: Portfolio
text:
This page highlights examples of reproducible data analysis and visualization workflows created using R and Quarto.



The figure below demonstrates a simple greenhouse gas emissions trend analysis using R and ggplot2.

library(ggplot2)

emissions <- data.frame(
  Year = c(2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023),
  Emissions = c(125, 118, 110, 97, 85)
)

ggplot(emissions, aes(x = Year, y = Emissions)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = "#2C7FB8") +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#1B7837") +
  labs(
    x = "Year",
    y = "GHG Emissions (t CO2-eq)",
    title = "Greenhouse Gas Emissions Trend"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)







Figure 1: Example greenhouse gas emissions trend









This section demonstrates simple data summarization using reproducible R workflows.

library(dplyr)

sustainability_data <- data.frame(
  Sector = c("Energy", "Agriculture", "Transportation", "Industry"),
  Reduction = c(32, 18, 24, 15)
)

sustainability_data %>%
  arrange(desc(Reduction))

          Sector Reduction
1         Energy        32
2 Transportation        24
3    Agriculture        18
4       Industry        15






The following code chunk demonstrates a basic reproducible workflow used in environmental data analysis.






NoteView R Code






mean_emissions <- mean(emissions$Emissions)

paste(
  "Average emissions across all years:",
  round(mean_emissions, 2),
  "t CO2-eq"
)

[1] "Average emissions across all years: 107 t CO2-eq"










Reproducible reporting with Quarto
Data visualization using ggplot2
Data manipulation using dplyr
Environmental data analysis workflows in R
Dynamic document generation

title: Curriculum Vitae
text: Embedded CV

title: About Me
text:
I am a graduate student in Ecosystem Science & Sustainability at Colorado State University with a focus on carbon management, environmental assessment, and environmental data science.
My academic background combines environmental economics, sustainability assessment, and quantitative analysis. Before joining Colorado State University, I studied economics and environmental-economic systems in China, where I became interested in the relationship between environmental sustainability, energy systems, and climate policy.
My current research interests include life cycle assessment (LCA), greenhouse gas inventories, carbon accounting, environmental-economic modeling, and sustainability analysis. I am particularly interested in applying quantitative and data-driven approaches to support low-carbon decision-making in agriculture and energy systems.

title: About Me
text:
Research Philosophy
I am interested in interdisciplinary approaches that integrate environmental science, economics, and data analysis to better understand sustainability challenges and climate mitigation pathways.
I believe combining quantitative modeling, environmental assessment, and policy analysis can help support more practical and evidence-based sustainability decisions.

title: Xinran Zhou
text:
Graduate Student in Ecosystem Science & Sustainability
Colorado State University
Focused on carbon management, environmental data science, and sustainability assessment through life cycle analysis, greenhouse gas accounting, and quantitative environmental-economic research.

title: Xinran Zhou
text:
Research Interests
My research interests focus on carbon management, greenhouse gas inventories, life cycle assessment (LCA), and environmental-economic analysis. I am particularly interested in applying quantitative modeling and data-driven methods to support low-carbon decision-making in agriculture and energy systems.

title: Xinran Zhou
text:
Current Research

LCA-LCC Assessment of Synthetic Humic Substance (SHS) Production and Agricultural Application

First-author research on four hydrothermal humification technologies for SHS production in China
Evaluating environmental impacts, economic costs, and process hotspots
Assessing mitigation potential under regional energy transition scenarios
Using Simapro, CHINA-SDU-LCIA, and province-level analysis frameworks



Greenhouse Gas Inventory Analysis

Estimating emissions using IPCC-based methods and process-based models
Conducting mitigation analysis in R and COMET-Farm
Developing scenario-based mitigation strategies for agricultural systems



Environmental Data Science Applications

Cleaning, analyzing, and visualizing environmental datasets in R
Producing reproducible sustainability analyses and technical reports
Applying statistical methods to evaluate environmental indicators and emissions trends

title: Research Projects
text:
Greenhouse Gas Inventory Analysis

Research Focus
Applied IPCC-based greenhouse gas inventory methods to estimate emissions and analyze mitigation opportunities in environmental systems.


Methods

IPCC Tier 1, Tier 2, and Tier 3 methodologies
Emission factor analysis
Uncertainty assessment
Scenario evaluation



Key Topics

Greenhouse gas accounting
Carbon inventories
Mitigation assessment
Environmental data analysis
